bullet_points: Load the dataset and estimate noise variances ($\\sigma_x^2, \\sigma_v^2$) using the Median Absolute Deviation (MAD) of high-frequency signal components to ensure robustness against outliers.
Compute the instantaneous observed energy and define the analytical Jacobian of the energy model with respect to mass ($m$) and damping coefficient ($b$).
Construct the Fisher Information Matrix (FIM) and compute the Cramer-Rao Lower Bound (CRLB) for each oscillator over time.
Perform sliding-window Maximum Likelihood Estimation (MLE) to estimate parameters $\\hat{m}$ and $\\hat{b}$ and their empirical variances.

Save all computed metrics (CRLB, MLE estimates, variances, sensitivity profiles) to a `.npz` file and generate a summary statistics JSON file containing the mean and standard deviation of the estimated parameters for each oscillator.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Data Processing, Noise Characterization, and FIM Computation

bullet_points: Load the processed data and summary statistics from Step 1.
Group oscillators into quartiles based on their damping ratio ($\\zeta$).
Calculate the Information Horizon ($T_H$) for each oscillator and compute the Pearson correlation coefficient between $\\zeta$ and $T_H$.
Generate a multi-panel figure showing the evolution of CRLB, empirical variance, and sensitivity profiles across the time domain, highlighting the transition to identifiability.
Save the resulting plots as high-resolution PNG files and print the calculated correlation coefficient to the console.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Visualization, Regime-Specific Analysis, and Statistical Correlation

bullet_points: Interpret the relationship between the damping ratio and the time-to-convergence ($T_H$) using the correlation coefficient provided by the engineer.

Explicitly compare the empirical variance obtained from the sliding-window MLE against the theoretical CRLB, identifying specific regimes where the estimator fails to reach the theoretical efficiency limit.
Discuss the validity of the energy dissipation manifold under parametric uncertainty.
Synthesize all findings into a formal Results section, summarizing the reliability of parameter estimation in underdamped systems based on the generated plots and statistics.
